---
type: fact
situation: When building a command-line application in Go
subject: Tests
predicate: are table-driven and use in-memory fakes instead of real files or wall clock
object: Define MemStore (map-backed, implements Store interface) and FakeClock (fixed epoch, implements Clock interface) in the test file. A newTestApp() helper constructs App with these fakes and a bytes.Buffer as the writer. Each test case is a struct with input args and expected output/error. Add t.Parallel() to every test and subtest. Use errors.Is to assert sentinel errors. Assert exact timestamps using FakeClock.epoch — only possible with an injected clock. No t.TempDir() needed for unit tests when MemStore is used.

---

Information learned: when in building a command-line application in Go, Tests are table-driven and use in-memory fakes instead of real files or wall clock Define MemStore (map-backed, implements Store interface) and FakeClock (fixed epoch, implements Clock interface) in the test file. A newTestApp() helper constructs App with these fakes and a bytes.Buffer as the writer. Each test case is a struct with input args and expected output/error. Add t.Parallel() to every test and subtest. Use errors.Is to assert sentinel errors. Assert exact timestamps using FakeClock.epoch — only possible with an injected clock. No t.TempDir() needed for unit tests when MemStore is used..
